original_hypothesis.title: Borrower Can Eliminate Fixed Term Protection by Setting endTime to Zero
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FixedTermLoanHooks.setFixedTermEndTime only checks that the new value is not greater than the current value (IncreaseFixedTerm check), but does not enforce a minimum remaining term. A borrower can set fixedTermEndTime to 0, immediately allowing all lenders to queue withdrawals since the check 'fixedTermEndTime > block.timestamp' in onQueueWithdrawal becomes 'false'. This eliminates the fixed-term guarantee that lenders relied on when depositing. Combined with APR reduction, a borrower could attract deposits at high rates, then immediately unlock withdrawals and slash rates.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The monotonic decrease invariant is maintained (any value >= 0 satisfies it), but the economic intent of fixed-term protection is violated. Lenders deposit expecting locked terms, but the borrower can retroactively remove this protection at will.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (newFixedTermEndTime > hookedMarket.fixedTermEndTime) revert IncreaseFixedTerm(); — no minimum value check, allowing newFixedTermEndTime = 0

preconditions: Borrower controls the hooks contract (immutable borrower role)
Market has active lender deposits with expected fixed term
Borrower willing to act against lender interests (trust model violation)

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Set endTime to 0 → all withdrawals immediately unlocked → combine with APR reduction to extract maximum value
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Borrower attracts deposits at high APR with long term, then eliminates term and slashes rates

economic_questions: Can lenders detect and respond to fixedTermEndTime changes quickly enough?
Is there on-chain notification when term is reduced?
Does the temporary reserve ratio mechanism provide sufficient protection when combined with term elimination?
validation_requirements: Verify all lenders can immediately queue withdrawals after
Test combined attack with APR reduction

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Proportional to loan size and rate differential

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After attracting maximum deposits at favorable rates
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete elimination of fixed-term guarantee for all lenders

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All lenders who deposited expecting fixed-term protection
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of expected term-based yield protection
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Interest differential across all deposits for remaining expected term

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test setFixedTermEndTime(market, 0) and subsequent withdrawal queueing

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
